version.approval_notes:
OpenPath Firefox extension review notes

Purpose
- Browser companion for managed OpenPath devices in education environments.
- Explains blocked pages/resources, supports access requests when enabled, checks local allowlist state through the OpenPath native host, and blocks known Google game surfaces.

Permission rationale
- <all_urls>: evaluates managed navigation/resource requests and applies path, subdomain, Google game, and local Windows dependency-overlay policy across web origins.
- webRequest/webRequestBlocking: detect and apply managed blocked navigation/resource decisions. On Windows, eligible subresource requests are reduced to anchorHost, dependencyHost, and requestType before local native-host validation.
- webNavigation: tracks the top-level anchor host per tab and clears per-tab state when navigation changes.
- tabs: scopes badge and popup data to the active tab.
- nativeMessaging: talks only to the local OpenPath native host installed on the same managed computer.
- storage: keeps managed config and local runtime state in browser storage.
- clipboardWrite: copies blocked-domain lists only after a user action.

Network/data behavior
- Manifest data collection declares required none and optional browsingActivity.
- User-initiated blocked-page and popup access requests ask for browsingActivity consent before sending request details to the configured OpenPath service.
- Google game blocking is local browser policy enforced through webRequest for known Snake, doodle-game, and interactive logo-game surfaces. A Google Search/Doodles visual guard content script locally neutralizes detected playable game widgets and sends only a best-effort local diagnostic message to the extension background runtime.
- Content scripts are limited to an isolated-world page activity relay plus the Google visual guard. There is no MAIN-world page-resource observer and content scripts do not relay AJAX/subresource URLs.
- Windows runtime dependency handling is local-only: the background runtime sends only anchorHost, dependencyHost, and requestType to the OpenPath native host. The native host validates against the local whitelist and writes exact-host Acrylic overlay entries. Dependency hosts are not sent to the OpenPath service and are not synchronized remotely.
- Firefox Core does not include Android support, remote automatic AJAX/page-resource allowlisting, or live/automatic AMO upload.
- No third-party analytics, profiling, advertising, or sale/sharing of browser data is included.

Source review
- Human-readable source is attached with the submission.
- See SOURCE_REVIEW_NOTES.md in that archive for rebuild and review notes.

release_notes.en-US:
OpenPath Firefox companion build for managed education devices.

This version includes the blocked-page/request flow, local OpenPath native-host integration, path/subdomain enforcement, local Google games enforcement, Google Search/Doodles visual guard content script, page activity relay, and Windows local exact-host dependency overlays without remote automatic allowlisting or automatic upload. It does not include third-party analytics or telemetry.
